cpSummary: Overwhelmingly parents (mostly mothers) treating school-age daughters with long/thick hair after back-to-school lice outbreaks. Buyers are stressed, budget-conscious, and have usually tried 2-3 failed products (pharmacy or natural remedies) before landing on the winner.

usageScenarios.label: Back-to-school infestation (maternelle/primaire)
usageScenarios.reason: Most reviews mention lice caught at school, often in September or after holidays

usageScenarios.label: Repeat infestation after failed pharmacy products
usageScenarios.reason: Buyers arrive having burned through 2-3 other brands first

usageScenarios.label: Whole-family treatment (mother + siblings)
usageScenarios.reason: One bottle rarely enough — parents treat themselves and all children simultaneously

usageScenarios.label: Long/thick/curly/afro hair challenge
usageScenarios.reason: Texture of hair drives product choice and quantity needed

usageScenarios.label: Preventive application
usageScenarios.reason: A subset use it proactively when outbreak announced at school

csSummary: Two sharply divided camps: enthusiasts who call it 'miraculous in one application' and frustrated buyers who report live lice after 8-12 hours of exposure — suggesting real variability in hair-coverage technique, resistance, and expectation management around nits vs lice.

negativeTopics.label: Product ineffective — live lice after treatment
negativeTopics.reason: Dominant 1-2★ complaint: lice still alive after full exposure time
bullets: Live lice found with comb after 8-12h overnight pose
Entire family retreated with zero result
quotes: "After 12h of exposure, lice are still alive — first time this has ever happened with an anti-lice treatment."
"I've done 3 treatments in a week — still finding live lice. 15 euros in the bin."

negativeTopics.label: Nits (lentes) survive — requires 2nd application
negativeTopics.reason: Even positive reviewers note a second round 7 days later is needed
bullets: Product kills adults but nits hatch days later
'One application' marketing claim contradicted by reality
quotes: "The lice die but the nits are another story — they come back a week later."
"One application is not enough despite what the box says."

negativeTopics.label: Cheap plastic comb — inadequate
negativeTopics.reason: Universal gripe: the included comb is flimsy, teeth too short/wide, breaks easily
bullets: Plastic comb breaks on first use
Teeth too short for long/thick hair
Users advise buying separate metal comb
quotes: "The comb is plastic, fragile, better to invest in a metal one."
"Comb broke at the first use — useless on thick hair."

negativeTopics.label: Too greasy — hard to rinse out
negativeTopics.reason: Silicone/oil base requires 2-3 shampoos to remove, leaves hair oily
quotes: "Product is like oil, I had to do 3 shampoos to get it out."
"Very greasy, protect floor and clothes during application."

negativeTopics.label: Irritation — burning eyes, scalp sensitivity
negativeTopics.reason: Spray drift into eyes, scalp reactions reported
quotes: "The product literally burned my son's eyes — he screamed."
"Scalp sensitive for a week after, even without lice."

negativeTopics.label: Too expensive / small bottle
negativeTopics.reason: Price perceived as high relative to pharmacy, bottle too small for families
bullets: 150ml not enough for long hair × multiple family members
quotes: "150ml is tiny when you have long hair and a whole family to treat."
"A real budget when you have kids in nursery school."

negativeTopics.label: Damaged / opened on arrival
negativeTopics.reason: Multiple reports of broken bottles, missing combs, or crystals clogging nozzle
bullets: Crystals formed in lotion block nozzle
quotes: "Bottle arrived open, product spilled everywhere."
"Full of white crystals, impossible to use properly."

negativeTopics.label: Overnight application too constraining
negativeTopics.reason: Some formats require 8+ hour overnight pose — hard on young kids
bullets: Kids can't sit still overnight with product
Rinsing in morning disrupts school routine
quotes: "Leaving it on a child's head for 8 hours is really constraining."
"Impossible to make a 3-year-old keep it on all night."

negativeInsights.finding: Marketing promises 'effective in one application / 5 minutes' but nits require 7-10 days to hatch and second treatment.
negativeInsights.implication: Expectation mismatch drives the majority of 1-2★ reviews — buyers feel deceived even when the product partially worked.

negativeInsights.finding: Included plastic comb is universally rated inferior to metal combs.
negativeInsights.implication: Every brand that bundles a plastic comb gets the same complaint — opportunity to differentiate with proper stainless steel comb.

negativeInsights.finding: Silicone/oil spray drift causes significant eye irritation, especially on young children.
negativeInsights.implication: Safety-label failure risk; claims of 'suitable from 6 months' contradicted by painful user reports.

negativeInsights.finding: 150ml bottles insufficient for multi-child families with long hair.
negativeInsights.implication: Family-size (400-500ml) SKU with better price-per-ml would capture repeat buyers.

negativeInsights.finding: 'Satisfait ou rembourse' 3-day refund window is seen as fraudulent — shorter than the treatment cycle.
negativeInsights.implication: Refund policy shorter than treatment cycle generates repeat 1★ reviews citing bad faith.

negativeInsights.finding: Puressentiel and natural-formula products under-deliver on heavy infestations.
negativeInsights.implication: Buyers who started natural end up returning to dimethicone-based products — positioning the natural product as 'prevention only' would set correct expectations.

positiveTopics.label: Effective in one application
positiveTopics.reason: Dominant 5★ theme: kills lice and nits in single overnight or 5-min application
bullets: Dead lice visible on comb right after rinsing
Saves weeks of fighting with other products
quotes: "One application and goodbye lice and nits — I recommend 100%."
"After months of fighting, this product saved us in one use."

positiveTopics.label: Best after trying many alternatives
positiveTopics.reason: Users explicitly compare against 3-5 other failed products from pharmacy
bullets: Finally found 'the one' after long battle
quotes: "After trying Marie Rose, Paranix, Apaisyl, essential oils — this is the only one that worked."
"The only truly effective anti-lice product I've found."

positiveTopics.label: Easy spray application
positiveTopics.reason: Spray nozzle praised as faster/cleaner than bottle-pour lotions
bullets: No waste compared to bottle formats
Quick to apply on squirming kids
quotes: "Much easier than bottle-format products — just pchit and done."
"Spray lets you dose precisely without waste."

positiveTopics.label: Short 5-minute exposure time
positiveTopics.reason: Compared favorably to competitors requiring 15-30min or overnight
bullets: No need to sleep with product
quotes: "5 min is top — works great, I recommend!"
"Much easier than overnight products for young kids."

positiveTopics.label: Cheaper than pharmacy
positiveTopics.reason: Amazon price routinely cited as half the pharmacy price
quotes: "100% effective and half the price of pharmacy."
"Hard to find in pharmacy, so grateful Amazon sells it."

positiveTopics.label: No harsh chemicals / family-safe
positiveTopics.reason: Dimethicone or essential-oil formulas praised as gentle for kids from 6 months
bullets: Suitable for toddlers and pregnant women
quotes: "Formulated without neurotoxic insecticide, acts mechanically."
"Suitable for kids from 6 months and adults — finally a safe option."

positiveTopics.label: Pleasant / neutral smell
positiveTopics.reason: Unlike vinegar or older-generation products, no chemical stench
quotes: "No unpleasant smell, kids didn't complain."
"Smells fine, not the chemical horror of other brands."

positiveTopics.label: Hair stays healthy after treatment
positiveTopics.reason: Oil base doubles as conditioner, hair soft after rinsing
quotes: "Hair is silky after, like a conditioner."
"Doesn't damage hair, good for long afro-textured hair."

positiveInsights.finding: Biggest advocates are repeat-customer parents who bought after 3-5 failed attempts with other brands.
positiveInsights.implication: Marketing angle: 'the last anti-lice product you'll ever buy' — position as graduation from failed pharmacy alternatives.

positiveInsights.finding: 5-minute pose time is the #1 differentiator called out vs overnight competitors.
positiveInsights.implication: Fast-pose format commands premium and drives 5★ reviews from stressed parents.

positiveInsights.finding: Dimethicone formulas consistently praised for physical (non-insecticide) action.
positiveInsights.implication: 'No pesticides / safe for 6m+' is a durable positioning hook as buyers grow anti-chemical.

positiveInsights.finding: Amazon price consistently 40-50% below pharmacy — key purchase driver.
positiveInsights.implication: Losing Amazon channel would collapse the category; pharmacy brands must decide whether to own DTC or cede to e-commerce.

positiveInsights.finding: Hair-conditioning side-effect (softness, detangling) appears as unexpected delight.
positiveInsights.implication: Lean into 'treatment + hair care' dual-benefit in the packaging copy.

buyersMotivation.label: Urgent eradication of active infestation
buyersMotivation.reason: Most buyers arrive in panic mode after discovering lice

buyersMotivation.label: Frustration with failed previous products
buyersMotivation.reason: Having wasted money on 2-3 brands, searching for 'the one that works'

buyersMotivation.label: Safe for young children / no chemicals
buyersMotivation.reason: Toddlers, babies, sensitive scalps drive formula preference

buyersMotivation.label: Cheaper than pharmacy alternative
buyersMotivation.reason: Budget pressure when treating multiple family members

customerExpectations.label: Kill 100% of lice AND nits in one application
customerExpectations.reason: Packaging promises drive this expectation — biggest disappointment source

customerExpectations.label: Short, practical application time
customerExpectations.reason: 5-15 min preferred over overnight; kids can't sit still

customerExpectations.label: Safe for kids from 6 months+
customerExpectations.reason: Non-irritating, no neurotoxins, no burning eyes

customerExpectations.label: Sufficient quantity for family/long hair
customerExpectations.reason: One 150ml bottle should treat multiple heads

customerExpectations.label: Easy to rinse / non-greasy
customerExpectations.reason: Users hate oily residue requiring 3 shampoos

reviews.t: Came open, looked used.
Came without the lice comb.
Ineffective product. The Mercadona one is better. Only kept the comb.

I don't recommend this product for kids — at least the lotion. The shampoo no problem, but the lotion has an unbearable, suffocating smell. If you buy it, use it with the bathroom windows and door open. As for results, you barely survive applying it to your child, and neither do the lice. Effective but dangerous.
Smells strongly of alcohol and doesn't seem to do anything.
The liquid was loose in the box, very crushed and open! The liquid spilled — the shampoo came fine!

I bought it on others' recommendation, but didn't realize it has pesticides and the smell is very strong. My daughters had a hard time and it doesn't kill lice on first try — I had to repeat after 7 days, obviously with another product! Won't buy it again — I prefer to pay more for one that's worth it and the kids don't have to bear that strong smell!
All items from the order arrived, but the four ZZ anti-lice boxes were open and 3 lice combs were missing — only 1 arrived.

Seemed great but turned out to be nothing — had to remove them one by one, rinse the hair with vinegar and tea tree in the cologne. The product did nothing for my 5-year-old daughter or me, who got them from her.
Very complete pack but in our case it wasn't effective 😔

The instructions are a bit imprecise. Doesn't specify whether to use the shampoo after the lotion or choose between them. Does something but not totally effective, even repeating the process a week later. The included lice comb has teeth too far apart so it doesn't fulfill its function well.
Doesn't seem to be effective. Four consecutive weeks of treatment and no improvement.
The product has a very strong smell and irritated the scalp. Was effective for eliminating lice but is too liquid to apply without it dripping onto the face, and the smell is unbearable.
I didn't like it because it arrived destroyed.
Just arrived and I still have to test it, but it arrived like in the photo — I don't think it's normal that it arrives open. Lamentable.

I only need the lotion because the shampoo seems rather bad to me, and my surprise was that they only ship if I buy the pack — they don't ship the lotion alone to the Balearic Islands but in pack yes. As I said, I like the lotion, not the shampoo.
Both parents and the daughter did the treatment. She and I still have lice. And it wasn't a big infestation, we noticed quickly. Have to repeat with another brand.
Kills lice but smells very strongly of alcohol. If you have little ventilation, jump to the balcony because it makes your eyes water — yours and the neighbors'. Won't buy it again.
When we received the product the box where the bottles come in was open and smelled strong.
I've bought it twice and she still has them — doesn't remove the nits which then reproduce again.

I was guided by the comments when buying it and at least for me and my son it didn't go well — 15 min with the shampoo on and then combing out all live lice. For that you might as well comb without putting anything on, same thing. In a couple days I'll use the lotion since I have it, though the instructions say the lotion is when there are no lice. I don't know, I'm not convinced.
Doesn't kill lice with just one use as the leaflet says. Another middling product. The lice comb isn't bad.
My order doesn't include a lice comb even though it's in the product description.
The product is great, fantastic. But the lice comb that's supposed to come as a gift... DIDN'T COME.
Works well but the smell is too strong, almost unbearable.
Not as efficient as I expected and very uncomfortable due to its intense smell!!
Doesn't fully eliminate the infestation in one application across several sessions.

I discovered lice eggs in my daughter's hair and ordered this to treat her. I knew it wasn't going to work because the comb wouldn't catch any of the eggs — just basically combed her hair. It took me a couple of hours sectioning off tiny sections of hair and spraying it onto the scalp, then massaging it in and spraying the length of her hair. Long story short, I did the meticulous process just to find more eggs in her hair. I have super thick wavy/curly hair and so does my daughter.
The item arrived open and all spilled out. The bottle was completely empty and I cannot even ask for a refund because there is no option.
Works a bit but definitely didn't kill all of them despite saturating hair in the product. Also be aware that as it's now in a spray bottle the oily liquid goes everywhere!
Not good at all — didn't even work, very disappointed with the results.
It's a great product but the price has gone up too much. That's ridiculous! It's now almost 3 times what I paid last time?
Bottle arrived open and the product had leaked all over the package, soaking everything in oil, inside the box and out. Barely any liquid left and there are no refunds or returns accepted.

The smell is so strong. It's extremely oily! I have washed my floors 5x and it's still so slippery! The bottles were leaking. Maybe change to a different bottle like what we would buy in store instead of spray bottle!

Paid $13 just to receive an empty bottle — kind of pisses me off. There was no indication that the box was wet and assumed all was well, only to find out that when I opened the box no comb, no liquid, just a dry empty bottle.

Didn't work despite the number of reviews stating that it did. Definitely need a comb to get rid of the little critters as I saturated my whole head for the first application without combing and thus it didn't work. Currently on third application. Not satisfied with the product at all as it's not cheap.
I had high hopes for this, but alas I was disappointed. It was easy to apply, but even when using the instructions to a tee, it just didn't work. Waste of money in my opinion.
Waste of time, waste of money, doesn't work.
This came opened and has leaked everywhere, including the outside packaging. A greasy mess.
The liquid was all over the place. There's nothing in the bottle. I got the package and the whole package is wet.
The bottle came open, most of the product was spilled.

Cons: Waste of money — it does not work. Smells strong despite being marketed as having a pleasant smell. It's also said reapplication isn't required as it'll do the job in one go for lice and eggs — unfortunately my toddler is still struggling! Now I need to spend on another product. Pros: though it's greasy it washes away easily.

The bottle had no packaging material to hold it in place and the entire bottle was broken open, completely spilled out, and empty upon arrival. Completely useless. Too bad because this is usually a great product otherwise — I've bought it before.
Didn't work at all for us.

Followed directions to a tee. Eight hours later I could feel the lice again. Reviews were good but product did not work for me. Used the email address in the box to try to contact the maker — it's not a valid address, bounced back. A frustrating disappointment.
Didn't work — treated my grandkids with this last week and they're back with me this week with more nits than ever. OMG this is a nightmare.

I don't know if this works or not (just applied today), but never ever again! This product is SO messy. The spray sprays not just the hair but everything around it. It's so greasy that it's impossible to clean off — there's always a small layer remaining. It makes the floor dangerously slippery and stains clothes with grease. It's literally everywhere. Even the shower is slippery!! The fact it's a spray rather than a lotion has not worked for us.
The bottle has nothing — it's empty.

Bought it from reading reviews but it didn't work. Put it in my kids' hair, waited even longer than stated. Nothing was killed — even the eggs weren't killed. The only thing that worked: I mixed conditioner with olive oil, put it in the hair, combed bit by bit using a lice comb. Washed hair with fairy liquid to get rid of the oily mess. Repeat every 3 days till all eggs are gone. Time-consuming but the only way.

In an ideal world there would be no such thing as head lice, but we don't live in an ideal world. When the little buggers make their appearance and decide to throw down roots in your children's hair, we all turn to the products that will hand them an eviction notice in the form of death. Unfortunately this product does not do that. It just gives a friendly knock on the door but then leaves. So the little buggers are happy out, alive & squatting away! Don't waste your money — just get a good l...
My scalp has been itching and irritated ever since using it, and red raw. Wouldn't recommend especially if you have sensitive skin.

We have purchased this item 3 times now because on the surface it seemed fantastic the first time around. We treated the whole household, the product smells pleasant (although it does leave a mess in your bathroom which can be slippery) and didn't leave hair overly greasy. However I literally combed live headlice out after this treatment had been left on 30 minutes (note the product states you do not need to comb), and eggs hatched in my daughter's hair after using the treatment.
Doesn't work — there are live lice on my child's head the following morning after treatment. Complete waste of time and money. As if nits weren't annoying enough!

Very over-rated and literally 3 uses, which isn't enough to get round the family. Slimy on wash-out and just not pleasant. Reverting to previous brand. Shampoo definitely the way forward and don't need half a bottle to get through hair.

Unfortunately I used this spray on my child & this is their skin reaction within a few hours afterwards. I personally had no problems or reactions but unfortunately my child did. Thought I'd let people know in case anyone else suffers from eczema as a precaution.

Unfortunately my child has someone in their class who isn't treated and keeps reinfecting everyone. We've tried all the products on the market as I read this is the best way to get rid if you catch them multiple times. Unfortunately, this was one product that simply didn't work. Followed instructions to the letter and I appreciate it smelled less bad than some, but it just didn't do the job. A total waste of money.
The product arrived in bad condition, there was leakage and missing product. I tried to return it but I can't because this product isn't refundable. My loss was big.
Bought this twice. First time we left it on for the guided 20 minutes, 2nd time we left it on for triple that. Neither time worked for any of us. Do not buy.
Disgusting — dirty used comb inside with what looks like head and pubic hair. Went straight in the bin.

I caught nits from my small granddaughters and because I wanted something quickly I ordered this product. Unfortunately it has not worked despite using the comb before and afterwards. This is the very first time a product like this hasn't worked for me, so I'm very disappointed. I have quite long hair so it's a mammoth task to redo it all today. I don't recommend it.

The 'one and done' promise is a lie. We used as directed and even kept it on longer while combing. A week later we still had lice that hatched out. Just buy the Sklice Ivermectin — it's pricier but Walmart has a generic ($30) and it's the only true one-and-done formula. You'll just be wasting time and potentially spreading with anything else.

Advertises that you don't need to comb the nits or lice out because it kills everything. I did everything directed including combing the hair and it still didn't do what it said it would. Treated my daughter's hair three different times every 4 days, did her bedding, changed her brush and hair bands — and she still had nits and bugs in her hair.

I'm now on my third bottle to try and rid my daughter's thick long hair of the things. I've caught them off her and used this also. It doesn't work and definitely doesn't kill everything in one go. There are cheaper alternatives that are better. I really think this product needs to be looked at properly as it's complete lies and false advertising!!! Do not recommend, won't buy again 😡😡

I ordered two of these and one of the bottles was completely empty and the other one was leaking all over the bag. Don't waste your money!! I'll use what's left of the bottle that wasn't completely empty and if it actually works maybe I'll add a star and update this review.

I was so hopeful this would work due to the good reviews but a week after using it an egg must have survived and hatched. Second treatment done as recommended, I'll keep checking with the comb every day. Shouldn't state it kills them in one go if it doesn't.
Absolute waste — nits/lice still present even after leaving on for 1 hour, comb included is also useless.
After applying longer than advised. Combed through hair for ages and have live nits still coming out of the hair and itchy scalp. Does not work in one go.
I received my package with two empty bottles and two damaged bottles.
Does not kill the lice after 1st treatment.
Doesn't work. I put one and a half bottles in my daughter's hair, left it on for the said time, and she still has live headlice and nits.
The product arrived damaged, all broken, completely empty because everything was shattered.

My daughter has had lice for over a month now. I've bought all the popular products off the shelf — I won't mention names but you know the big ones. I found this and thought YES! I was convinced this would work. I've applied twice across 3 days plus combing, even though it says combing not required. My heart sank when I saw eggs glued to her hair strands. I'm now beside myself and don't know what else to try.
It was leaking when I opened the box so I never got a chance to use it!

This treatment was very oily — you couldn't tell that the hair was saturated and then it dripped everywhere leaving the floor slippery wherever it dripped or sprayed. On your skin it penetrated leaving skin feeling greasy. Washing it out left my niece's hair smelling like a wet dog and oily.

Has the consistency and appearance of oil, hard to wash out the hair, the scent is sickly and made me gag to apply to my son's hair, and seemed impossible to get completely washed away. Having checked his hair again after seeing him itch I can see eggs still — meaning the treatment didn't kill both lice and eggs and I'll have to start again. If I could claim a refund I would!

First & last time I have bought a chemical head lice solution. I had high hopes after reading some reviews but despite leaving the product on for longer than suggested, it had no effect. Only good as a standard oil to slow down lice & make it easier to comb through hair. Eventually got rid of lice & eggs by using my usual method of thorough repeated combing with a Nitty Gritty comb. Save your money & don't try to be lazy.

Read a lot of good reviews and was very hopeful that it would work. Tried it twice with a gap of 1 week, followed the instructions, but it didn't work for me at all. So disappointed. Either the product was expired or I don't know what but I still have a full head of lice.

Bought this after the rave reviews. Did a whole bottle on daughter's shoulder-length hair and combed through afterwards to remove all traces — but a week later she started itching and more eggs were discovered, so another bottle ordered. Same thing again. So after £22 and two doses it didn't work for us. Will go back to other brands that did work.
I've used this a few times now but each time it's taken two applications before working. This time even after two my daughter still has nits. Supermarket trip now.

Don't waste your hard-earned money buying this product. I purchased this for a friend who wanted it to treat hair lice, thinking it'd work based on the great reviews. Cost us more in the end because we had to buy something else.
When a teacher sends a kid home with lice, this works well.
When this arrived, it arrived empty — all of the solution had leaked out due to the cap being loose, so nothing was left in the bottle and it had run on everything else in the box.
The package came defective and empty.
This product was already opened and used... whatever was left over in the spray oil bottle was leaking. So gross!!! Not eligible for return or replacement. Don't buy!
I hate to leave a negative review but it's absolutely useless. Don't waste your money.
Received a box that had already been opened and product had been used. Bottle was coated in oily product and there was hair and sac in the comb. Gag.

I don't like leaving negative reviews but we followed directions and actually left it on my son's head for 30 min. The next day we still found live lice on his head. Third product we've tried and have read there are insecticide-resistant lice now (super lice). So we're doing it the good old-fashioned way: tweezer-picking them out.
Tried twice, doesn't work at all, disappointed.
The bottle came open and spilled all over the box.

Tried various other nit killers and hoped this one might work. Fat chance — the spray didn't work. Later when opening the bottle there was no tube attached to the spray pump. So I'll never know whether it works — yet another disappointment.

Unfortunately I wasn't able to use the product at all because the bottle arrived completely empty. Very disappointed to open the package and see the liquid spilled all over. Even more disappointed to find I couldn't get a refund for a product I never got to use.
My product spilled over.
Eggs were still alive.
Arrived open with all the product spilled.
Seriously oils the head and hair and the little critters just swim in it. They don't die!! This does not work. We had to order another brand.
The funny thing is they can take the money in seconds but I have to wait 7 days for my money back.
Had to do the second treatment as I saw mature head lice after one treatment.
It really didn't work — the brush didn't take anything away. Also it was greasy and nothing fell off and I had it on for 30 min. Didn't see anything different.
This didn't work.

I bought this after reading the positive reviews — wish I hadn't wasted my money. Treated my son (very stressful, lots of tears), left it on about 30 mins and used the comb too. Two days later he's still riddled with nits. I'll have to buy something else now. Very dissatisfied.
Don't waste money. Did absolutely nothing and wasted money to turn around and buy shampoo that does work. Days of extra misery for all. Don't waste money.
Awful — does nothing, just like grease on the head. You comb and a few come out but it doesn't really remove anything. That's my experience.
Hasn't worked at all. Son still has loads of nits. Large ones too, so not newly hatched eggs. Disappointing as no option to return for refund.
Super disappointed — bought 4 boxes for our family as my daughter had a confirmed case and it doesn't work, not even a little. Followed instructions to a T, didn't kill off anything.
The package was opened, the liquid was all over the place, the bottle was almost empty.
I followed the instructions, saturated the hair, combed before and after, and 2 days later my daughter still had nits in her hair! Way more expensive than other brands and really rubbish.
One-star reason: open carton and missing comb.
Left on for an hour, combed thoroughly, washed off. 10 minutes later the hair started itching again. So no, does NOT get rid of lice. Waste of money!
I don't understand the good reviews! This product simply doesn't work. The included comb is a joke — nowhere near any other on the market that can remove eggs. Don't waste your time and money.
Unable to use as when it arrived the box packaging was wet and the lid wasn't on the bottle properly and it had all leaked.

Doesn't even deserve 1 star... waste of money. Not sure how people give this product such good reviews. I'm reviewing after long-term use, doing exactly as per instructions, but it didn't make any difference whatsoever. Useless product, wouldn't recommend to anyone. Worst product ever.
Followed instructions to a T! Combed as I was washing it out and they were still alive. Also went through after washing and there were still some in her hair alive. Did not work at all!! Rubbish.
Used this on my son's hair once and it didn't work. Used it on my own twice — still no success. Have moved on to different products.

Super easy to use, but after two applications in one week it failed to remove the lice and eggs. On both occasions we left the product on for over an hour for maximum effect. In our experience a complete waste of money.
Used this for my daughter. Didn't work. A week later I noticed she's still itching, checked her hair and she has more nits than before. Wouldn't recommend.
When I picked my package up from the driveway I saw it had leaked all over. When I opened the package I saw the bottle was empty. So disappointed. Waiting for my replacement.
I bought it because of so many stars. Used it. Was terrible and didn't work at all. Came back to the 1-star reviews — read them all and agree with every single one. Super expensive rubbish product.

I'm not one for writing reviews but was surprised by all the positive reviews. This product absolutely did not work for us. Tried leaving it on longer and combed hair thoroughly, which it states isn't necessary. Bought two bottles and used 3 times on my daughter's long curly hair. Had no effect on the nits/eggs at all! Maybe it works for some but was a complete waste of money for us. In future I'm sticking with Lyclear.
There's no comb inside when I received it. The system won't let me refund or replace. Very disappointed.
Followed the instructions, having made a horrible mess everywhere, but it didn't work. I still have the little blighters.

I bought this based on the great reviews. My son had picked up nits from preschool. This appeared to have a higher percentage of positive reviews. Application was straightforward (albeit oily!) and I thought we'd cracked it when several dead nits slid out. Alas, my happiness was short-lived as a few hours later he was back to scratching despite following instructions. Now attempting to eradicate them with neem oil. Wouldn't recommend.

Very disappointed. It says it kills lice and nits — kills lice yes, but nits no way, that's a lie. In the end I had to buy the Mercadona product which works better because its lice comb actually drags the nits, which the Nitwits one doesn't.
Used product. Original box broken and comb sticky from the liquid itself. A disgrace.
Can someone explain how to open it? I couldn't get the cap off any way and it doesn't specify. Can't use it because I can't open it, and ended up breaking it. What a bad system 🤦

Not worth it — money in the trash. When I put it on my daughter I thought at least it didn't smell as bad as others, though it's very oily, and I had hopes it'd deliver on its promise of 100% elimination. Nothing — my daughter still has lice. Won't ever buy again.
Didn't work and costs quite a lot.
Tried it on my daughter and it didn't work. Money down the drain.
The product arrived spilled.

Can't comment on effectiveness as my daughter didn't have headlice — used as a precaution after a reported case in her class. The product itself is such a mess! The mist gets everywhere, makes everything oily and slippery. The carpet is now super slippery! Her hair was so greasy after thorough rinsing and 2 shampoos. Had to wash again the next day. Not sure I'd use this again due to the messiness.
Not good.

One of a long line of nit treatments which didn't work for our super-resistant head lice. Easy to apply and didn't smell off-putting, but the only product that worked for us was the Superdrug own brand.

2 stars. Doesn't remove the nits — you still have to pick them out. Second time I had to do my daughter's head because of the false advertisement on the bottle saying no need to pick. Now her head is way worse than it would have been. I don't even think it kills the lice or we wouldn't be going through this a second time.

I bought this All-In-One Head Lice Treatment Spray hoping it would solve the problem, but it didn't live up to the claims. Used it 4 times for my daughter following instructions carefully but I still see lice eggs in her hair. It might help with live lice but nothing seems to work on the eggs. Very frustrating and disappointing.

The product does appear to get rid of nits which is great. The smell is like a pesticide and very strong unlike others on the market, and very very greasy — we had to wash the hair another two times after and wipe all surfaces.

Product is good. Packaging is bad. I had 1 bottle arrive empty and saturated the box. Amazon replaced and the 2nd shipment leaked 1/3 bottle. We still used the product. It works. But you need to put a seal on the bottle.
Haven't used it yet so don't know efficiency, but it came half empty as the bottle must have leaked. Pros: pleasant smell. Hopefully the amount left will be enough to treat.
Didn't work. Comb teeth too far apart so doesn't get the eggs out.

I bought this as my daughter had a major lice infestation. After one round the big lice were all killed however the nits and tiny lice still remained — a huge job to clean out. Possibly the spray would have to be used multiple times but it does not clear/kill lice and nits in one go as advertised. Make sure to keep using a fine comb and checking after this treatment.
Bought 2, both were opened with box tape seal cut. Doesn't work very well — product may have been watered down or replaced. Either way I won't buy it again.

It works but absolutely destroyed my hair. I have long hair almost down to my waist. Used this because my hair was too long and thick to get a nit comb through and thought a spray would be easier. It worked and I was able to use the nit comb and my hair is clear. Since washing it out my hair was tangled, damaged and broken — couldn't get a brush through. I've since used 4 different intensive conditioning treatments.

I so wanted this to work as the reviews are great. Tried Hedrin spray once and the small tube already and couldn't get rid. Easy to use but the comb is rubbish. After leaving on for just over recommended time, not only could I still see them but could feel them moving. Trying Full Marks next.
Used this 3 times but hasn't worked. Followed directions and after washing out, combed through and still found live nits each time.
I don't think it's helping.
Not very effective at killing lice. Used this product on my daughter and eventually had to resort to a specialist.
Lovely smell but didn't work. You simply can't leave without combing! Ended up treating with another brand as the head lice hadn't gone at all!
It clears the lice but not eggs — eggs are still there and the comb doesn't work well.
This is so false!! Doesn't kill eggs and clearly not live ones either. What a waste of £17. Should not be allowed to make false claims.
Despite following instructions, numerous live lice still combed out. Leaves hair very greasy.
Spray is a waste and a gimmick. And the bottle is so small for the price.
Rating packaging mostly here rather than the product. The package was damaged and the bottle was leaking. Very disappointed.

I really don't understand why it has good reviews. It does smell nice but it doesn't work — when you comb the hair everything gets stuck in the comb and there's no way of cleaning it unless you rinse every time. Had to purchase a metal comb at Walgreens which works way better than the plastic one.
Killed the lice but not the eggs. Normally when eggs are dead they go white or yellow. Used this and they were still alive, still brown and dark. So disappointed. Smell isn't that nice either.
I'm not going to use a product in an open box on my child!

Killed some of the nits but not all. I found live nits in my daughter's hair the next day and we followed the instructions to the letter. The nit comb is rubbish too. After two main brands not working 100% I stuck to traditional conditioner and combing every day for a week.
It says it kills lice and eggs in one go but we've used it several times to actually kill them. Also doesn't kill the eggs. Eventually they died.
Takes several applications. Still not sure if it's working. Doesn't state what percentage the dimethicone is.
Gave my kids a chemical burn on their heads.

Smells nice and doesn't drip, but very hard to wash out so wouldn't recommend for long hair. Not as effective as other brands. Comb that came with it isn't as good — teeth are really short so not very useful on thick hair.
Better than others but still doesn't work well on lice. It's a lie that one use removes the lice — should be used 2 or 3 times.
Waste of money.
Purchased because of good reviews. First time I thought I hadn't used it properly so used again — still no result.
I applied this product on my daughter — lice are gone but eggs are still there. Not happy at all.

Ordered after reading great reviews. This product kills the nits as stated but DOES NOT kill the eggs and you definitely need to use a nit comb with it. With the 2nd application I left it on my daughter's hair longer than the 20 mins hoping it would kill the eggs the first hadn't touched, but it hasn't worked. Won't buy again or recommend.
Smells nice, easy to apply, doesn't get rid of the lice or really reduce them either. I was disappointed. Wasn't expecting a miracle cure but something better than this for the price.

Hasn't worked. Was easy to apply with protection but my daughter who kindly brought the crawlers home from school for us to share is still scratching, as am I! Have to try another brand. The school nurse tells us the lice these days have become bionic.
Smell is strong, doesn't come off even after a few washes. Product says no combing needed but comes with a comb.

This product is quite effective but very oily and takes 2-3 washes to remove. HOWEVER, I've been experiencing severe hair loss after using this which has caused me severe anxiety! Has anyone had the same experience?
Simply didn't work. Don't waste your money.
I specifically searched for a non-shampoo one and this is not that. Takes up to 4-5 washes to get out. So incredibly foamy — time-consuming and annoying. Avoid.
Used twice and didn't work so ended up buying Nitwits.

Really easy to apply but didn't work completely for us, and we followed all directions correctly. My son was still scratching the next day and we found a live one. We left the solution on overnight as well. Fine-combed twice before treatment too. Washed all bedding and towels on hot. Back to combing now. Hate the things!
Easy to use but didn't get rid of nits and I did use a nit comb (this product says no need to). Yet to find a product that works. Just using conditioner and combing them out.
This did not work for my daughter or myself. Even after two applications she still had headlice. I resorted to combing and using neem oil which was far more effective.
Thought I'd give this a go but nope, doesn't work. Scent is nice — that's about it.
Unfortunately, after 5 weeks with regular use once a week and using the comb, there are still lice in my hair and my daughter's. I think the lice resist the treatment.
Not sure about this product as since use I have an area on my head that keeps scabbing. This is the only new product I've used.
This stuff absolutely stinks. Made my daughter gag for the full 20 minutes. Didn't kill everything either. We've used better stuff for a lot cheaper.

As far as products go this is decent — it's a spray so easy to apply. Only on for 15-20 minutes. It's like oil and coats the hair making it slippy so ideal for using a nit comb. I have yet to find a product that works without combing every piece of hair. This is meant to kill them all in one but I really don't know — kept finding them after treatments.
Nice smell. Easy to use. Didn't kill them all. Lyclear is better.
Leaked in transit.
Did not get rid of the eggs even after applying 3 times.
I liked the comb and Nitwits was pleasant and easy to use. However, like all the others I've tried, it failed to kill these resistant little blighters.
From reading the reviews I thought I'd give this a try but unfortunately didn't work for my daughter.
It hasn't removed the lice for me. For what it costs I expected more. The product is oil-like. Doesn't leave the hair like a scouring pad or smell of chemicals but isn't effective.

Works well but is very expensive for the quantity. I was looking for one with dimethicone since it's the best and safest for kids — it works by suffocating the lice and isn't chemical. It was the only one arriving in a day and I needed it. But I found it expensive.

I used this back in 2024 when I somehow caught nits after 24 years of being nit-free. It worked great then first time round and I got rid of them. So happy with the product. However those little buggers returned October 2025 and I went back to this kit. Did three treatment rounds and still couldn't completely get rid. Don't know what I'm doing wrong but this treatment did not work for me this time.
Smells a lot better than other treatments but very very greasy and hard to get out of hair.

I was happy to order a spray treatment, having previously used liquid you apply directly. Thought it'd be more convenient. However, days later I'm still trying to clean the bits of spray that landed on my floor and counter. The floor became super slippy and dangerous. Sprayed much closer than the instructed 10cm and spread by hand, so don't know how else I could have avoided this.

Check the box — mine was missing the brush! I used it but couldn't brush out the lice. So mad. I looked for one with a brush included. Used it on my daughter, went back to get the brush and it wasn't in the box.
Not the best. Helps but not with eggs.
Damaged.

Not sure if the ingredients have changed but the white-and-black bottle with the blue nozzle works amazingly well and the infestation was gone. 5 months later my daughter brought them back and I had previously bought another bottle just in case — however this bottle and box were different (white and blue) and when used this time they did not remove the infestation.

Works pretty good. I'd leave it in overnight or for 24h. Then comb and wash. Repeat in 5 days and again in 5 days to be sure. I've had lice 2 times and they're very hard to get rid of. Kapow is very good too. Leave it 24h. Sleep in it. Put a plastic cover on head and tie it tight. 24h will kill most if not all live bugs. Repeat every 5 days at least 3 times. Good luck and be patient.

This was OK to use but didn't really work. Very oily, so put a towel down if you have wooden floors! A decent comb is needed to manually remove all the little critters. You can't rely on this spray alone unfortunately.

I left this on for 20 minutes and combed for an hour straight, and the large headlice were still alive but struggling. Several days later I repeated and there were lots of young lice, so it clearly didn't kill the eggs. A week later I checked and loads were left and I had spent 2 hours combing. 4th time all gone — combing is definitely necessary.

It works, but unfortunately my package arrived and most of the contents had spilled out so I was only able to save about 1/4. Smells good and very easy application. Just wish I could have saved some for another time.

I bought this because my son's headlice kept returning. Smells quite nice. Very oily and only stays on the hair for 20 min. I did comb through but we're now on 4th treatment as I've just seen more eggs. Disappointed that it's pretty much same effectiveness as any other.
Works OK but I'm not convinced it kills the eggs too. Ended up having to use this three times before I finally got rid of it all.

This didn't seem to have much effect on the headlice. We all still had them a week later. Had to use a different brand. The texture was good, the bottle was smaller than I thought. Did last on 4 heads.

Been battling head lice the last 6 months with my 2 daughters. Tried every product, every comb, and still headlice was coming back. When I read the reviews on this product I was so hopeful. First, it says no need to comb — I combed after leaving the product 1 hour and found plenty of headlice. Says no follow-up treatment needed — 5 days later checking again and still some, so doing the treatment again. Only thing I really like is how shiny it leaves the hair.

I know this treatment is supposed to kill eggs and lice, not necessarily get rid of dead eggs, BUT I've now done three treatments, followed instructions each time and I still have an itchy head. I thought my scalp was just recovering but I'm finding more and more eggs as well as that telltale crawling sensation 😭
Didn't work as expected. Had to go through 3 different other treatments before all the lice nonsense stopped.

I've bought two bottles and treated my daughter three times over the last month, and despite regular combing I've found 3 nits again tonight. 2 popped, so obviously not goners! I'm losing the will to be honest and had high hopes. My daughter has thick waist-length hair so perhaps they're managing to hide. Either way the b*****ds are still residing on her head!
Satisfied with the product.

Beware with this one — whilst it does seem to get rid of the nits it can cause eye irritation. You also need to cover the floors, particularly wooden ones, as the floorboards will be covered and you'll be squeaking around for days. You need someone to apply this for you and keep your eyes closed and open all the windows afterwards.

Smells nice and is easy to use. Just a shame that it doesn't actually kill the nits as it claims. I've had kids at primary and nursery for 17 years so periodically have these critters visit. 1 nit in my youngest's hair lived through the complete process and came out with combing very much alive. The best way is still showering, leaving conditioner in and combing with a Nitty Gritty.

This product, like many others containing dimethicone, can present a serious slip hazard. It's a spray and if you have a tiled floor like ours in the kitchen and bathroom, you find that even the tiniest amount on the floor creates an invisible hyper-slippy sheen. Not water-soluble, so even scrubbing with soap or repeated rubbing doesn't get it off. Just about every member of the family has nearly fallen over. Last night my wife nearly fell.

Product works really well but both original and replacement product are leaking and have oily residue on the bottle before you even open it. Granted, the second bottle's leakage isn't as bad as the first.
Protect all nearby surfaces as the spray goes everywhere. Kids still have lice despite following the instructions on the box, so I need to do the treatment again.

Bought this to treat my daughter's hair after reading good reviews. Quick delivery. Found the bottle kept slipping out of my hands during application. Washed out fairly well and not too greasy. Bought a separate comb and painstakingly went through her hair in small sections. Disappointed to find the nuisances back in no time despite my efforts.
Had to do 3 treatments and a neem oil rinse.
Read the previous reviews but it wasn't so fast — had to do a second time. Drips a lot. Found it messy and greasy. Wouldn't buy again. Just not suitable.
Daughter's school had an outbreak so figured I'd be prepared. One box arrived opened (you can see the unopened one has a seal), so keep that in mind ordering.

When I first ordered it, it worked wonders. So I decided to buy two together as I have thick long hair. When I tried it the second time, it was a failure. My other bottle is just lying in the cupboard, packed. I'd recommend it if you have less lice, but if it's worse, don't buy. Nits don't go away as the description says.
We don't like it and it's too oily.
Works well killing lice that have hatched but isn't able to penetrate all nit eggs. Hair should still be combed through with a Nitty Gritty to ensure they've all gone.

Amazing formula, done the job well. However, when I tried to use it, it wouldn't spray. Tried again and the black part snapped off and broke! I have reusable spray bottles in my house — I put the formula in them and it did a great job. Recommend purchasing the Nitwits formula, just a bit disappointed in the bottle breaking!
Good.
It did not work! I used it twice and it still hasn't worked. I used raw peppermint.

Received this today. My daughter didn't have a bad case of nits — never used solutions before as she has a sensitive scalp. After washing off and shampooing I found 2 live walkers and live eggs, so I'm very disappointed 😞
This keeps head lice at bay — fair product.
Doesn't do what it says.

Bought this as it has very good reviews and is supposed to kill all live and eggs in 20 minutes. So I applied it to my daughter's hair and decided to leave it on overnight to make sure it works as best as possible — but to my dismay eggs were still alive. Unsatisfied.

The cap on the bottle wasn't securely tightened so some of the liquid had leaked. I needed the product so had to go ahead and use it rather than request an exchange. Too early to know whether it worked. Very strong and rather sickly floral scent and didn't wash out easily, leaving my daughter's hair very greasy.
Couldn't get rid of the lice with this product, however it helped slightly.

I've always used this on my kids and normally it's amazing — I always recommend it. But this time it hasn't worked. They've changed the bottle colour so I don't know if they've made other changes to the lotion itself, but I'm not impressed.

Smells good and isn't an oily mess like others. Easy to use and only 2 washes to get it out, unlike others that need 5-6 washes. However, even with fully saturated hair we still found some live lice after 45 min. Doesn't kill eggs. Comb given was useless so I reverted to my old faithful Nitty Gritty comb.
I had to use an entire bottle and the comb broke easily. However it isn't too expensive and in the end it worked, so I can't complain too much. It washed away in the shower without any problems.

This product works but has leaked right through the delivery box. Lots of other comments also mention the bottle arriving leaking. The lid wasn't screwed on properly inside the sealed Nitwits box. This needs to be sorted!
Was a lot easier to use than others, smell isn't bad and a lot easier to wash out — only 2 washes. The only downside: after 3 hours on, still 2 alive! You definitely need to use the comb.
Doesn't work as expected. Used it a few times but lice still there.
OK, the product itself is good. However I only got half a bottle!!! The lid wasn't on tight enough — was very messy when taking it out the box! The pictures don't really show how bad it was.
This product does not kill the eggs or lice. It just makes hair greasy so it's easy to comb and remove lice. I've used it twice this year and still had to go through heads and remove the critters.
I normally use Nyda headlice treatment which is great. Thought I'd try something else — unfortunately this isn't anywhere near as effective as the Nyda one. Also took 5 days for delivery.
Disappointed. Smelled better than other products which my daughter preferred but it didn't work, so 1 week later I had to buy a different product. Stick with better well-reviewed brands.
I liked it, very easy to use. Just not sure if 20 mins is enough to kill the nits.
Would have been 5 stars but came leaking and didn't get the most from it. Will definitely use again — like I say, it leaked all in the box.
Maybe we have a super-resistant form of hair critters but this didn't do the job for us. Going back to daily combing ☹️
You do need to use a nit comb to get eggs out — they will not just wash out.
I order this from time to time to protect my hair but this time it arrived without the small comb.
Good product however did not get rid of all nits on the first wash.
The product did not kill the lice however made it easy to remove.
Three members of the family had nits — the lotion killed off two infestations but my youngest child still has them.
With me, doesn't work. After bath there are too many lice still.
Good.
I had to use it few times.
OK.

Nits are a nightmare when there are young children at school. One of my sons seems to be the nits' favourite person and they're giant specimens. Finding a treatment is hard. They're all oily because that's how they work — this is no exception. It did work but we also kept his hair short to prevent repeats. I still think it's the combing not the treatment that has the most effect.
It's a shower curtain. Not sure what else to say. Looks like it's supposed to and stops most water in its tracks. Buy it or don't.
I used it in summer because at the hotel park there were lots of children and the girl came home itching — and good result.
Worked perfectly on first application.
Of all the ones I've tried for my girl this is by far the best, but... but the comb is useless and doesn't pull out the nits — unlike the lice which fall on their own when washing the head.
Came with very little liquid but it removed lice and nits from my daughter.

After our third lice outbreak announcement this school year I was ready to shave everyone's heads. Then I found this spray miracle. One application followed by the included comb has sent every nit in our household packing. The best part? It's simple enough to use on all the kids without starting a mutiny. My only complaint is the trigger handle seems irresistible to my children.

The all-in-one head lice treatment is an effective way to remove lice. The only downside is the oiliness — needs a thorough shampoo to remove properly. Overall provided relief for an itchy head. Good value for money.
Had to apply a couple of times to see results. The comb is useless — had to buy another from pharmacy. However I do recommend the product.

My daughter had a lice outbreak at school and we were short on money so this was the cheapest I could find on short notice. It does kill everything on her head as promised but it does not kill the eggs. Smells OK. The comb was a bit of a joke. After buying metal ones later, much the same. So we used this and everything died, then we saw more lice in her hair.
Need to use twice to completely rid of the nits. This also leaves behind a bit of residue which can be hard to get rid of.

I tried the NitWits all-in-one lice spray for my household, and overall it did the job, but not quite in one go as advertised. The spray is easy to apply and much less messy than traditional treatments. After the first use it definitely reduced the lice but it took a couple more treatments and thorough combing to fully get rid of everything. Also gentle enough for both adults and children.
I teach kindergarten and kids too often come in with lice. I keep this on hand and do a treatment when they come in with them. I like how gentle it is on my hair. So far so good.

I bought this spray because I needed a fast and effective treatment against lice and nits. I was surprised how easy it is to apply: the dispenser sprays well, doesn't drip, and the smell is tolerable. After the first use we already saw a significant reduction, and the included comb made removing the nits easy. The best part is it doesn't leave my hair too greasy.

Pros: Quick results — first treatment showed great results with many dead lice found after combing. The itching stopped right away. Effective over time — after the second treatment I only found baby lice (probably from eggs that hatched). Gentle formula, non-toxic, pesticide-free. Cons: leaky bottle.

My daughter had a head full of lice again. Usually I rigorously comb twice a day but bit sick of it this time so thought I'd give this a try. Although instructions say combing beforehand isn't necessary I did it anyway. I already had a good metal comb which is fortunate because the one they're supposed to give was missing from the box — that's why I knocked off 1 star.

Got most of them killed and hair was not greasy afterwards. Had to repeat treatment again in 4 days to get the remaining ones. I have waist-length thick hair and it worked. The comb it comes with is not good — better to use a separate metal comb.
Good.

Really easy to use and the smell is quite pleasant. However, unless you have severely dry hair you won't get away with just one shampoo following treatment. The product is oil-based and leaves hair extremely greasy. I've washed my granddaughters' twice and they wouldn't let me do it a third time. Take care not to allow any spray onto a hard floor.

You may need to follow up with a treatment for dry itchy scalps as we found it irritating. Would definitely use again as it works well and is easy to use. Wash out first time with washing-up liquid as it is hard to wash out.

After reading the reviews and trying Rid and Nix with minimal success, I tried this. The product is not packaged properly. I had to order it twice and each time more than half spilled out. I managed to have just enough between 2 bottles to treat my daughter's super thick hair. Other products left live bugs but this one seemed to kill everything. Quite a greasy product.

After using Full Marks solution and getting rid of the adults, we found the youngsters and eggs had survived so a different approach was needed. This stuff killed the young lice and despite saying you don't need to brush — you do. Easily removed with a Nitty Gritty comb. Nicer smell than others but very messy when applying.
Good but dries the hair.

My daughter has really long thick hair and I've used 2 other treatments in the past which haven't really worked. This one is oily but easily washes out. Definitely kills all the lice but time will tell if it killed the eggs. So easy to put the nit comb through her hair before I washed it out. Smells nice too! I left it in for an hour and a half just to make sure. Will definitely buy again.

I did three heads including my own and used the whole bottle. Very effective except for one adult egg-laying bugger. It came out of my head — I took it out with a nit comb and the louse was still wriggling. I sprayed it with the lice treatment and it was still moving! How this superbug survived I don't know. Not sure if eggs have been killed — will check in a week.
I think this worked pretty well.
Loved this product, it really does work. Just wanted more product for the price.

Updated to say I used this again as daughter has got a new case of nits. Wasn't so impressed this time as we combed it out and found two live lice still. Hopefully it will have done the trick as before. Very pleased with this overall. It is greasy stuff and using the spray is messy.

The product itself is good. After my sister had come round for half term she caught lice from my neighbour. I did this treatment and it works great, but to be sure do a second one about a week later. The comb they give is rubbish — best option is to use a metal comb you can get from Boots.

I probably just didn't wash it out right but it leaves your hair so greasy it looks like it's wet!! I'm not sure if it got the nits out but I haven't had any itching yet! Very good though I will say :)

I purchased this twice. Overall I used 6 products. Not sure if this was the winning one or the fact I had a routine in delicing my daughter's hair. Advice: wrap the hair in a bag. I left my daughter for the time stated and when I checked in she looked like she'd been completely greased up ready for the fryer.

Overall I'd recommend this product. I have very long, thick (Asian) hair so I knew it'd be a challenge. After trying a few failed methods I used this. It did kill all the eggs and lice however not all came out when I washed the hair. Took me a few days to get them fully out. Smell is lovely but very messy and took a few washes to get the product out.

A nice little spray bottle with a small comb. Quite straightforward to use — spray everywhere and leave for 20 mins then wash out. It says no need to comb after. I did the steps above but combed my daughter's hair while she was in the shower. Many lice and eggs removed but noticed the same night that a few eggs were left. Will repeat.
Product worked good at getting nits out. But get a small brush to get them all out.
Helped eliminate problem.

This lice shampoo worked well but applying it left the room rather messy. I'm surprised there's hardly any mention of that in other reviews. Because it's a spray, the spray doesn't stop at the head but goes past it, landing on the floor, the wall, whatever's in reach; it also drips from the hair after application. We didn't realize at the beginning so the whole floor is covered in our footprints and spray marks.
It killed about 80% of the lice — not too sure about the eggs, time will tell. It didn't leave my daughter's hair greasy, washed out easily and has a nice odor.

I've used countless products trying to eliminate head lice from my daughter's hair! This stuff is the only one that's actually worked. It doesn't smell too bad either. As it's a spray it seemed to go further too which is handy. It does take quite a few shampoos to get out, but I found putting a good dollop of shampoo directly onto it before water helps. Top product.
The second application and all were gone but the nits need to be taken out.

My daughter has stupidly long hair and was itching like mad, so I bought this just in case although I hadn't seen any nit evidence! Instructions easy to follow and doesn't smell vile like some treatments. Washed through afterwards and saw one thing that might possibly have been a head louse but I'm not certain. Washed her hair through again the next day, definitely stopped itching.
Excellent product.
Worked.
Worked like a charm — haven't had any reoccurring problems since. Dropped a star as it says just wash out, but actually hair has to be combed otherwise they lay dead in your hair even after washing.
Very easy to use, doesn't smell bad, and makes it easy to comb out.

Great product. Certainly seems to get rid of them after two treatments (initial application and one week later). Worked for my 6-year-old daughter and myself. However we got them back so had to re-administer. Whether that's because there were eggs that didn't die and hatched, I don't know. But I trust this product.
Good smell, easy to use. But for me too oily. Works well.

Doesn't smell as unpleasant as other products. Difficult to apply to myself as the bottle got very slippery so I ended up just pouring it over my head. Easy to apply to the children. Has to be washed out with a LOT of shampoo. Didn't affect the colour in my hair which is a massive bonus.

Used this for my 2 daughters — works, but don't recommend doing it as a one-time treatment. Still found some brown eggs in the hair so will repeat in 5 days. I put the treatment on then combed my child's hair while I waited. Recommend an hour and definitely comb to get as many eggs out as possible.

I've knocked off one star because it gets absolutely everywhere which makes it a nightmare to clean up and leaves anything it touches a dangerous oily slick — especially worrying if used in the bathroom in case of slipping. Other than that, does exactly what it says, although has a very strong scent.
Works a treat, worth a try 😁

The smell is not too strong. The oil is so strong I had to wash my kids' hair 2 or 3 times with shampoo to get the oil out. The bathroom floor was slippery after the treatment — I had to mop it a couple of times. I will not buy this again.
This works — cleared the nits in one hit.
Thanks.
