The bottle advises using 'detergent' to wash it out — which says it all! Took three washes with Fairy Liquid to strip this out of my 14-year-old son's hair. He was in agony — scalp extremely sore. Avoid at all costs.

Not one to write negative reviews but this is important. If your hair is dyed or a little dry DO NOT USE THIS! It's ruined my hair — broken, dry and badly damaged. Plus gave both me and my daughter dandruff. Used as directed.

Followed all the instructions. Got some nits and eggs out but the nits were alive and they came back. Not worth the price, no different to cheaper versions. Only real solution is to cut the hair short.

Horrible and greasy. Like honey/treacle to put on, doesn't all come out once washed so I'll have to rewash the grease out. Very messy and goes everywhere. Comb was awful — luckily had a metal one. Would not use this product again.
Wouldn't recommend sadly. SO GREASY! Took about 5 washes over several weeks to get hair back to normal.
This is what happened to the nit comb after the first use. Horrible plastic that broke on first use.

Bad quality, unable to comb through hair as the lotion is a thick, greasy, tar-like formula which stains and sits like OIL on everything! A nightmare to clean off bathroom tiles. Wish I'd never bought it but fell for the 'as seen on Dragons' Den' comment like a mug.
Impossible to actually wash out of hair, also strips out hair dye colour.
Absolutely inadequate. Didn't work twice in a row. The second time I even left it on for over an hour.

So very disappointed. Catchphrase says 'made by a mum who's had enough of head lice' — but this product needs to be kept on for 2 hours!!! Plus the writing on the product is so small I had to download a magnifier app to read the instructions. Impossible to get my 4-year-old to sit around for 4 hours.

Don't normally write reviews but this stuff is absolutely horrible. A thick oozy gel hard to get to the roots. Takes a few washes to get out but the worst part is it gets everywhere in your house. We've washed our floors and hallway three times over three weeks and it still squeaks and is slippery.

After months of different treatments I thought this was the answer to my prayers. However, this has done nothing to get rid of my head lice. Followed all instructions and left it in for an extra hour. At first you think they're dead, but it only stunned them for a few hours. Don't waste your money.

I've bought a few different brands and didn't like this one at all. The gluey texture was difficult to apply, the nits had to be brushed for ages and still not all came out. Had to re-wash hair several times. Other brands are much easier to deal with.
No brush, no box. Just the product. Product is so strong it made painful cuts on my fingers. I even bled and I used gloves. They should warn you about these things. It even damaged my scalp.

I bought 2 bottles of NitNOT to treat myself and my son. Followed instructions to the letter, thoroughly coating each strand. Left mine on for 3 hours and my son's for 2.5 hours. Frustratingly I still have headlice. Would not recommend — it does not work.

Applied lotion to hair as directed and left in for the full 2 hours. Repeated treatment and even on the second application the lice were still there. Costs double any other treatment and not effective at all.
Completely ineffective! Bought mid-March, applied 2 times to my daughter and myself for over 2 hours. All over the head, cling film on top. The lice are still very much alive!
After using this product my eye swelled and was incredibly itchy and still is a week later. The product had a weird texture but on the plus side my hair isn't greasy.

Tried this — no obvious headlice but a child in my daughter's class has them so I did us all to be safe. Absolutely impossible to wash out. I tried washing-up liquid as advised on the bottle but hasn't helped much. Very disappointed. Won't use this brand again.
Expensive and not good — like putting glue in your hair.
I used twice and the little pests are still there. The oil is good and washing-up liquid does get the oil out, but no, it didn't work.
No recommendation — it's like an oil hood. You can wash your hair several times before you get the stuff out!!
I left it on for more than an hour and eggs and lice came out of the comb very much alive. Doesn't work.

After reading reviews I really thought this would work. Used on my kids' hair and my own. After combing in the bath I noticed faint movement from the lice — even though we'd had this product on for hours. Means it doesn't kill the lice! It did make them easier to get out though.
Horrible texture — like smearing liquid Vaseline on your hair. If you have thick hair like my daughter, it's painful and virtually impossible to comb through. Will never use again.

This has completely ruined my hair. After washing with washing-up liquid, shampoo and conditioner, my hair is still greasy no matter what. Don't be fooled — it's a complete scam. Yes it gets the lice out but leaves your hair ridiculously greasy. Wouldn't recommend at all with fine or light hair.
Unfortunately.

This was awful. Smelt awful, sticky to the point I couldn't get it out no matter how many times I washed. Eventually gave up and had to wash their hair repeatedly every day for a few days. Also didn't work — still had alive moving nits after combing.

The product works, BUT there's no information how to get the liquid out of the bottle. There's a plastic insert which has to be removed with a sharp knife or pliers. Top is a screw/lock type which isn't obvious. Please include instructions on the back.
This product did not work as claimed and is very oily, with a sugar-like texture not easy to remove. Only good thing was no smell. Could have gotten same result from a bottle of baby oil.
It's an oil not a shampoo — greases all the hair, takes at least 3 shampoos to remove. I'll see results on lice tomorrow but I'm itching even while drying. Won't buy again, don't recommend.
Took 10 shampoos to remove the greasy product. Like having a jar of lard on your hair and hands. I'd give it a 0.
Despite following the instructions step by step and doing 3 applications, the lice and nits didn't go away! Plus expensive. The comb broke — the teeth bent making combing painful.

I used the product as described. Spent 1.5 hrs combing my daughter's hair with a nit comb — live nits in her hair again 4 days later, so it does not kill the eggs as described. Waste of money! I'd really like a refund.
Gloopy and horrible to apply. Took a full bottle of washing-up liquid to get it out of two heads. Leaked everywhere. Bought because it was eco-friendly but overpriced and impractical.
Unfortunately it didn't help, even though I gave more time than necessary.
Did two treatments with this on thick wavy hair. Took several attempts to wash out and it didn't kill the lice.
My little girl only had about 5 tiny nits. When we combed the product through after an hour, they were all still very much alive.
Was very sticky — very hard to get a comb through hair once this is on.

Unfortunately doesn't work. Followed instructions (oil trickling down face for 2 hours wasn't pleasant). I felt movement in my head the following day. Did see empty eggs in my son's head and was able to comb out. Reapplied several times then moved on to other products. 2 bottles made this very expensive — and didn't even work.

This used to work but now after 2 full applications (both of us used half a bottle each application, totally soaked, combed through, with bag on head for 4 hours each time), I just found eggs and a huge nit in my daughter's hair. Used to work a treat but not anymore — they're resistant.

Does not work. I'm a social worker and constantly have to use nit products. Decided to try a different brand and this was awful. Followed instructions fully — a waste of my money. Will request a refund! Go for the classic cheaper brands.
I'm so annoyed — it's so important to (work).
Normal, very greasy.
The bottle leaked inside the package.

Surprised by the number of positive reviews. Have children with near waist-length hair. Consistency is like castor oil or honey and took nearly a whole bottle per child (and has to be repeated a week later). Left it on between 1 and 3 hrs. As you're combing through, the lotion sprays and drips everywhere.
Effective on flatmate's straight hair. Matted my own wavy/curly hair. Great when you can actually comb through it, awful when you can't.

This didn't work well unfortunately on my daughter's thick dark long hair. More of a serum and we normally use an oil. The serum was so sticky and thick it was challenging to comb anything out. Clogged the comb.
Doesn't smell bad but very hard to rinse. I don't recommend.

Can't comment on effectiveness as it was more precautionary, but I needed to leave a comment to say this stuff is LETHAL if you get it on a hard floor. After applying to my little boy, he must have had his head on the floor because the floor was so slippy — my little boy and other child slipped over, and then I slipped over myself 🥴 All floors mopped but it still feels slick.
Didn't work.

I regretted using it as soon as I put it in my hair — knew it'd be a nightmare to wash off. I used it in my own hair to make sure I hadn't got lice from my child. So sticky and greasy I now don't want to use it on my child — he's autistic and hates having his hair washed. Waste of money.

Italian instructions hard to read, stuck like an adhesive together with other foreign languages — couldn't peel them off. 2 inner seals hard to remove. Greasy, oily product — after 3 washes with my usual shampoo still greasy. Not sure if lice are dead but my hair definitely is greasy. Their advice on the website is to wash with dish detergent.

Thought it killed nits. It only kills the lice not the eggs — next day there were lice. Treated again and the following day, lice again, newly hatched. Will look for a different option, probably the cheaper Hedrin.
Very greasy. Took 3 washes with washing-up liquid to get out which elongated the whole unpleasant process. After 3 weeks the nits returned, so unfortunately didn't work either.

It reliably kills the lice but is absolutely ineffective against the nits (eggs). You have to do multiple treatments, like with all other products. In that case you can just use cheaper home remedies, since they work just as well.

Bought two of these in a mad panic to sort out my and my daughter's nit problem. But 3 weeks in and the nits are still going strong. Smells nice but is hard to wash off your hands. Came off easy with shampoo. I do believe it killed some nits, hence the 2 stars, but I had to go with a different product.
Didn't work at all, plus it's incredibly greasy!

I had hugely high hopes for this. Splashed the extra cash, followed all instructions, combed through after use. Felt really positive at how sticky and different this product was — however the nits were still crawling when the whole treatment was done. So disappointed, particularly when it claims 100% effective. They still crawl post-treatment — that is not 100%.

I really disliked the texture which I'd compare to liquid glue. The instructions on the bottle literally recommend using washing-up liquid to remove — and I can see why. I'll use it because it was expensive but won't buy again.
I thought it'd be more effective having read the reviews but I didn't get on with it. Plus it's hard to wash out because too sticky.

I absolutely lathered mine and my kids' hair in this and followed instructions — unfortunately live lice were still moving after treatment and combing. Very thick and difficult to cover every strand. 2 stars for one good thing: it made the eggs comb out very easily but unfortunately they weren't dead. Going back to my tried-and-trusted baby oil.
Doesn't work.

Given the reviews I expected a product that would definitively solve the lice problem. Unfortunately it wasn't so. Price about in line with others, perhaps a bit expensive. Poor packaging. Oily texture requiring lots of rinsing and that drips during application. After two applications I still found nits despite combing carefully. Won't repurchase — found much better products.

I saw this on Dragons' Den and thought it was a great idea — supposedly easy to use and natural. Actually it will never come out of your hair and you'll be using a lot of white unnatural detergent. Also goes all over the face and soft furnishings. The comb is also a colour which means you can't spot anything.

Currently on the 3rd go (as instructed — 2nd treatment a week after first, 3rd 7 days later). Still finding nits and not hopeful it's working. Also just found out you can get free 'Bug Buster kits' on the NHS, so don't bother with this until you've tried that!

It is very very gloopy. We did as instructions said. I have now washed my hair over 13 times, 8 of those with dishwasher liquid, and my hair still has the product in it. I would not go for the hassle. I was excited about it being eco but I've tried better eco products.
I used this for 25 minutes first time, a week later for 2 hours and a week after that for 20 minutes. Still there is evidence of activity.
Quite expensive and unfortunately didn't work. Easy enough to wash off. Comb is useless.
The included comb is absolutely not the one shown in the product video — it's just a simple plastic comb. Once applied the product is very difficult to remove.

Kind of works but not amazing. On daughter's long thick hair it does kill some lice but never gets rid of them completely. Takes close to 3 hours for the whole process. I'll stick with mainstream products or just conditioner and a nit comb.
Comb is missing with delivery and you can't return this item or complain to receive the replacement. Not good.
Followed the instructions perfectly and it did not kill the adult lice — only killed the young lice. Did not kill the eggs either. Have to leave on for 2 hours.

Arrived late. Arrived with product leakage. Doesn't include the comb. Pose time is very long and for kids it's hard to keep the product on the head for two hours. Says to brush with a simple comb but if you don't use the specific comb the nits don't come off! So you have to buy it separately and the total cost becomes high. Not at all miraculous as advertised.

Used this on my 5-year-old, preferred to choose non-chemical, left on for the stated 2 hours. The washing-up liquid did work to get it out as extremely oily. As I was washing out and brushing, lots of my daughter's hair came out. Never had this before — I'm concerned.

I was a bit disappointed by the result. Since my daughter was full of nits and not lice I thought I'd solve the problem more easily, but despite leaving the product on for two hours, after washing the hair I still found almost all the nits attached!! In the end I had to comb the hair strand by strand!!
We tried this 3 times and although the numbers reduced it didn't get rid of them. I suspect the combing did most of the work. Also extremely messy.
When the item arrived the plastic covering had been half removed and the lid was loose.
The application — the lid isn't really straightforward without ripping your teeth open.
Anti-lice shampoo — barely effective.
I've put it on my little girl's head twice and resolved nothing, even leaving it for more than two hours. No result — after a few days back to square one. Don't recommend.
Not so good. Lice are still there.
2 hours is a weird amount of time to leave it on, also very sticky.
Impractical product. Compared to other treatments it's really too oily — to the point you have to rinse several times with dish soap. Personally don't recommend.
This didn't do anything — oily OK but still itchy. Washed my hair so much. I used olive oil that helped me more. Waste of time and money.
School sent a ping alert... hope this works... too early to tell.
Hated this stuff. Way too sticky, like honey. Wouldn't buy again.
A bit disappointing that the serum needs to stay in the hair for two hours. This information was mentioned in the product info on Amazon.
Arrived leaking and not full.
Used 3 times as instructed but nothing — ineffective!!
The product probably does its job but it's really very oily. Even after shampooing the hair is weighed down. There are less greasy products.
Didn't help much despite multiple applications.
A bit pricey product.
Initially was a good product but after a while becomes less effective. Maybe something to do with potency or the hair getting used to it.
If you have autistic, ADHD or sensory-avoiding kids — avoid! It works, yes, but wasn't worth the meltdowns!!

Be warned — this is an extremely messy treatment. I recommend applying while sat in the bath, as the towel around my shoulders made no difference and it dripped everywhere. Oil-based, our bathroom floor was slippery for weeks despite cleaning thoroughly. Also needed a second treatment because my hair is so thick and it didn't kill all the adult lice.

Update after 2nd treatment: I followed up a week later. Along with more eggs I found one very large adult louse. Seems to have worked eventually. With two kids in infant school, lice are constantly making their way back. Pros: no nasty smell. Found this possibly the worst I've tried to date.
This is a nightmare to remove from your hair after five washes with, yes, as they suggest, washing-up soap. However it did the job.
Used 2x according to instructions — unfortunately eggs and nits survived.

My baby came home from nursery with lice so I wanted something less chemical as he wasn't even one yet. Honey-like texture which was fine but it started dripping as I left it on for 2h to be safe. I've now done this 3 times and although I can't see them, on the 7th day we started itching again. Eggs aren't fully removed. The included comb is a joke.
Mediocre.

After the 2 main pharmacy treatments doing nothing I bought this. What a nightmare. Took 2 hours to apply to my daughter's long thick hair, then it has to sit for 2 hours before washing — that's where the fun started! 2 washes with T-gel didn't shift it. I tried fairy liquid as instructions recommend — nope. I panicked. Eventually 2 washes with purple shampoo worked.
Good stuff just very greasy and it took 4 washes to get it out of my hair, and still isn't 100 percent. Used as a precaution and didn't find anything so can't comment on effectiveness.

I used this on my son, left it on for 2 hours then washed off with washing-up liquid as instructed. Hasn't worked — tonight I'm still pulling live ones out. Comb is rubbish, only killed about 40%, and yes it's seriously greasy. Top also wasn't working so I literally had to pour it onto his head. Bonus: doesn't smell at all.
Impeccable.

Not very effective at all. Slimy texture, not a serum. I smothered my hair in this and didn't have a big infestation. Left it on for 1 hour. Combed it out thoroughly. A day later still crawling with lice.

A few weeks ago I noticed a few lice. Pharmacy gave me Hedrin 5-min — didn't work. Then 8-hr Hedrin — still saw a couple alive. Combed daily with conditioner and decided to try NitNot. The day after treatment I suddenly found lots of white bits on my girl's head — not sure if dead eggs or about to hatch.

I can't really judge effectiveness — must be excellent — but it's IMPOSSIBLE to wash off. I did it on my daughter, after she was totally water-repellent. Two shampoos and she's still all greasy. Not sure how I'll send her to school tomorrow. Impractical.
All good.

On the plus side, easy to comb through hair when this is on because so greasy (so not too much whining), and if you use washing-up liquid as instructed it doesn't leave hair greasy or ravaged like some chemical products. On the negative — miss one tiny egg and you're back to square one. I was extremely thorough but the blighters live on.
The product itself is very good but the comb provided wasn't very helpful. Had to use a separate metal comb to get rid of lice.

I waited months to post this review. This product is fantastic. My middle daughter was heavily infested with head lice and nits, and this did the job after three applications. I had to buy three bottles for our four-girl household with thick curly hair. No scent, no mess, safe for young children, easy to wash off. Highly recommended.
Instructions not in Italian.

Reading enthusiastic comments and after spending a lot to eliminate these hateful parasites, I bought NitNot. At first it seemed fantastic — seeing many dead lice I said 'finally we're done!' After leaving it on for 3 hours and washing with dish soap, I passed the metal nit comb and spent the whole day at it. Then changed all sheets etc, thought I was finally done after a year — but back to square one.

It got rid of the extra friends, but I'm not sure if that's because it was so greasy and thick it stayed in my son's hair for 4 days, and we were washing it twice a day to get rid of it. It only seemed to go once he went for a swim in the sea!

It spreads in the hair like craft glue. You have to wash it out with lots of normal shampoo at least twice in a row. It works well though — nothing can survive in the gluey mass. Smell is fairly neutral.
This stuff is sooo thick and it didn't work. Takes a few washes to get out due to the thickness. There are definitely better treatments out there.

My son came home with a few headlice and I tried this product after seeing the reviews. Very thick consistency that almost feels sticky. It does kill headlice. However my back where this product touched is bright red, sore and itchy. Second treatment, itchiness worse — I realised it was the product. Wouldn't purchase again.
Greases enormously, hard to rinse out.

Awful stuff. Easy to apply but thick and very greasy. Hard to keep on the scalp! Left it for hours and washed out. Shampooed three times. Combed out a couple of dead ones, but 90% were still crawling. Went to another company and killed them all. Don't waste your money.
Does the job but leaves scalp very dry.
After the first application it didn't completely eliminate lice and eggs.
It is kind on the scalp but not enough product and too expensive for one use.
I ordered this as it said kills fast, but you have to leave the solution in for 2 hours, which is not ideal with little ones! Otherwise a good product.

It was OK — killed the live head lice. It said it killed the eggs — not true. We waited seven days and used again, but in the meantime we did her hair with a nit comb and eggs were still cracking, so still alive.
May be a good product but very oily. On my daughter who has long curly hair, rinsing and combing was a drama.

Not bad, it does work, but really sticky and uncomfortable. My only problem: I did what it said, sat 2 hours with my little girl with it on her hair, but every week she seems to end up with them again.
Good product but disappointed it arrived broken which made application hard and messy!

This product was so easy to apply but ridiculously hard to wash out. My daughter washed her hair and still had the product in despite rinsing multiple times. It wasn't until the following day that the product was removed. We followed all instructions but had difficulty.
Torn with this review because it didn't work on my kids' hair. HOWEVER it's the easiest one I've ever used and definitely the easiest to remove. Best product I've bought — pity it didn't kill them.
The product is good and does its job, even if not on first try, but it's too oily — I couldn't get the greasy effect off even with dish soap.
Unfortunately I didn't solve it with this treatment! Even though I was very careful.

Did many heads in a house with a few children but it's so greasy it was difficult to get out. Ended up washing hair in washing-up liquid and still greasy after six washes. It did however get rid of the problem but I've had better with less hassle.
I wasn't entirely satisfied with this product. Unfortunately didn't catch everything. Hard to spread and at the end hard to wash out. Didn't convince us.
Not bad but it took more washes than expected to remove the parasites. The included comb (too delicate) broke easily. Lucky we had the metal one.
Kids feel it's too strong on their heads — good for over 17s.
Used a second time, I don't know why it doesn't work for us. Saw good reviews but didn't get rid of them. Would prefer to have a comb too for the price.
Too oily a product for our needs.
Let's say we had to use it two or three times to completely eliminate the lice.
Would not recommend. Extremely oily and runs down your head — have to leave it on for 2 hours minimum and doesn't come with a comb.
Good fragrance, smells like peach tea. Can't say if it really sends lice away. Leaves the hair a bit greasy.
Review of product & updated star rating to follow once I've tried the product twice 7 days apart. Impressed with seller's customer service.
It was OK to remove.
Yet another failed attempt — hard to use on kids because very thick, and didn't solve the problem anyway.
After just over a week, the eggs are back.
Not the best for this money.
Very good.
The product eliminates lice but not nits. So after a few days the lice reappear.
I expected much more from a product that has to be left on for two hours.

I used this on my daughter who has very long hair. Happy to say it's enough on the bottle for all her hair. Took a very long time as you have to leave it on for 2 hours, then it's tricky to wash out as thick and oily. Smells OK, not too strong like other brands and seemed to do a good job. Be aware it says to repeat 7-9 days later, so you might need two bottles for longer hair.
We've been using it together with the comb and it seems to have worked, but you have to use it for a while.

VERY thick lice product. Barely flows, so not good for long hair. Very good for short hair. Doesn't smell. Tip: wash out with dish soap. Then it was gone after two cycles. Don't forget the second treatment after about 8 days!

With two kids in primary school and one in nursery, I've been battling lice for years and multiple times per term. I'm resigned to treating lice at least once a month. After trying literally every product on the market, not a single one is 100% effective first time. If you're expecting one-and-done, you'll be repeating.

I used it on my daughter who got lice at school. It worked. I left it on for an hour and a half. It does leave the scalp very greasy — I didn't dare wash with dish soap as suggested, so I used my usual shampoo and after two days redid it and the grease was gone. I also bought the metal-toothed comb from the same brand.

Very effective! My daughter is a lice magnet. After trying tons of products and grandmother's remedies I tried this — almost nothing left! I give 4 stars because I'm angry: the product is so greasy I stained a chair and my floor was very slippery. I fell on the floor.
Works well.

Excellent product! Easy to use and does the job. The only let-down was the comb — when I first bought this, it didn't come with a comb. Now it does but it was awkward — became really slippy as you comb the product through and kept slipping out my hand.
Easy to use but very oily. The comb was very good. My problem: very difficult to wash out of the hair even with washing-up liquid.
Killed the bugs but took 4 washes to rid the grease.
Wasn't completely satisfied with it.

I used the product on my own head (49-year-old mom) and on my daughter who gave me the lice. She had a real infestation. I applied the very oily product to cover all the hair, dividing into sections with a standard comb. Then I started immediately checking section by section with the small comb. Most were dead — but three lice were alive and other live nits. Didn't eliminate everything.
Extremely effective at relieving itchy scalp but it removes moisture from hair and requires washing-up liquid to remove the lotion quickly. Effects last 1 week to 9 days.

Used 1 bottle for a mild infestation for 2 of my children, myself & husband. (2 of us with long hair.) Easy to apply, like spreading runny honey through your hair! Left it on 40 mins then nit-combed in sections. Kitchen roll needed as it gets messy. Washed out with washing-up liquid first, then shampoo — worked well. Bought a 2nd bottle to repeat at 7 days.

Compared to what I read in reviews, the box says the pose time is at least 20 minutes (not 2 hours). Does its job. First rinse with dish soap helps remove the greasiness. Easy to apply, a bit more demanding to rinse.
Worked really well.

After trying a few other products and battling head lice for a while, this did the trick. However it was the messiest to apply. My daughter has waist-length thick hair and one bottle was only just enough for one treatment, and as two treatments are required we needed another bottle. My daughter has sensory difficulties and found keeping it in for two hours hard to tolerate.
Satisfactory product.
We tried dozens of products. No miracles but better than the others.

I'd tried a lot of products on the shelf and none had worked! Two months after a huge battle, daily combing and conditioning, tea tree oil, olive oil and god knows what else, nothing had worked. I tried this and immediately felt a difference. Nits and eggs came out much easier than with other products. I still found baby nits after washing — just kept combing.

Did the job well. But very sticky and takes a while to rinse out. If your child isn't keen on having hair washed it may be a bit much. Suggests washing out with washing-up liquid but that isn't ideal for a child with eczema.
Really thick.
Although they say you shouldn't order such products online, this is a really good product. After one application I had no more lice.

Works as advertised! No idea where I got them from but around two weeks ago I managed to end up with lice. (No children, live alone — goes to show you have to be careful as an adult.) The majority were dead when I combed them out after 2 hours, however there were still live ones. Doesn't work on eggs so apply once a week for 2-3 weeks. Feels like oil and washes out well.

I came back from holiday and discovered I was infested with lice. The bottle recommends using twice which I did, but it got them all out with the first use. Recommend using a nit comb to brush them all out just before showering. After the recommended leave-in time I did see some still alive so I left it in another hour and they were all dead. Hard to wash out.

Very effective, it works. Easy to use and my hair was even left feeling softer and hydrated. More than enough product in the bottle. The comb was too frail for my thick hair however and I had to use another.

My 10-year-old gets nits all the time. This is the least toxic-smelling and easiest to apply of all the products we've used. Go straight to washing-up liquid to wash it out — shampoo just doesn't cut it. Seems to work with shorter applications. The plastic comb lost a few teeth on first use so we stuck with our own metal-toothed nit comb.

Good product, seems to work following the package instructions. Price a bit high but worth it if 100% effective. Rinse a bit long and laborious as the product creates an oily film on the hair, but with patience washes out. No smell and hair doesn't seem damaged.
Excellent product, does its job as suggested.

After reading the reviews I bought it. Did my 2 girls' shoulder-length hair, but didn't have any left for mine. Most reviews said the product goes far — well maybe they got a bigger bottle. Apart from that, did a good job, combed all nits/lice out after an hour. No smell — great! The comb that comes with it is the best one we've ever had.

The consistency is an oily gel — thick enough to coat the hair but not sticky like other gels. By the time we'd washed the hair, all dead lice were falling off. Lost a star because difficult to wash off. It does suggest washing-up liquid but I didn't dare use that in my hair.

I bought this for my daughter as a precaution due to an outbreak in her school class. She used it once — easy and straightforward application. No noticeable scent, not greasy on application. Wouldn't hesitate to use again.

Yes it's liquid silicone. You'll need washing-up detergent to get it out. 2 hours twice, a week apart. Plus combing. Still didn't work. Used a different, quicker brand on the child which worked better, albeit with more chemicals.

Applied well and seemed to do the trick but agree with others that it's sticky to wash out — did it twice with shampoo and hair still a bit greasy, third time it was clear. Next time I'll try the washing-up liquid trick!
We use it as prevention. At school many kids are catching them — we still haven't. So for now it's working.
As a product, may even be top, but the cost is too excessive. With one bottle we only did 4 uses — once for each family member.
The product works but is quite oily and requires several washes to eliminate the film.

I'd give it 5 stars but despite following instructions carefully and smothering the hair, 1 was still ALIVE! Actually quite a good product though rather oily. Not bad but might need tweaking to make sure those pesky blighters all die!
